title: dicranopteris seramensis
intro: Dicranopteris seramensis, also known as the Seram glade fern, is a species of fern in the family Matoniaceae. It is a perennial plant native to Indonesia, particularly on the island of Seram. Dicranopteris seramensis has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, decoction, and powder, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
